title: Misato dialogue in EoE
wikitext:
[]: Misato dialogue in EoE             Quote Post by Eliaskar » Fri Feb 19, 2016 4:31 pm I can't seem to find it, but I remember hearing that the dub mistranslated her explanation about the angels to Shinji in EoE? I can't remember what was wrong about it, so could somebody enlighten me?

[]: Re: Misato dialogue in EoE             Quote Post by Arcadia's legacy » Fri Feb 19, 2016 4:34 pm I believe she says that Lilith was created of Adam as opposed to being a separate being herself. And iirc, the subtitles make this error also

[]: Re: Misato dialogue in EoE             Quote Post by pwhodges » Fri Feb 19, 2016 5:31 pm Not quite - she says that Adam came from Lilith; the same error is in both dub and sub. EoE subtitles wrote: They had to reduce Adam to an embryonic state before the other Angels awoke. You see, Shinji, mankind was spawned from a being called Lilith, just like Adam was. We are the 18th Angel.

[]: Re: Misato dialogue in EoE             Quote Post by Arcadia's legacy » Fri Feb 19, 2016 5:51 pm D'oh

[]: Re: Misato dialogue in EoE             Quote Post by Reichu » Fri Feb 19, 2016 5:55 pm It's an understandable mistake, all things considered. シンジ君。私達人間もね、アダムと同じリリスと呼ばれる、生命体の源から生まれた、１８番目の使徒なのよ。 Shinji-kun. Watashi-tachi ningen mo ne, Adamu to onaji Ririsu to yobareru, seimeitai no minamoto kara umareta, 18-banme no shito na no yo. Aside from the fact that I have no idea what a "ne" is doing after "mo" (and will thus ignore them; fortunately, they don't have significant impact on the meaning) , this is overall a pretty straightforward and typical Japanese run-on sentence. It breaks down like so: [Shinji-kun.] [We humans mo ne ], [called Lilith (which is) like Adam], [born from a source of life], [are the 18th Angel]. Which, rearranged to fit English grammatical patterns, becomes something like, "We humans are the 18th Angel, born from a source of life called Lilith (who is) like Adam." You can smooth that out to "a source of life like Adam called Lilith", if you prefer. Now, this is (slightly) easier to parse when you're looking at it written down with all the commas there, making it a bit more clear that "Adam to onaji" ("like Adam", "same as Adam") is being applied to Lilith. If you're just translating by ear, as Manga Entertainment's people probably were, then there are no commas and you're forced to take everything as a whole. In which case, it is possible to read "Adam to onaji" as applying to everything that follows. And since the ultimate subject of what follows is "18th Angel", we ended up with the mistranslation, "You see, Shinji, mankind was spawned from a being called Lilith, just like Adam was. We are the 18th Angel."

[]: Re: Misato dialogue in EoE             Quote Post by robersora » Fri Feb 19, 2016 6:55 pm I'd say, the translation is more like Like Adam and Lilith, Humanity has sprung from the same source... We are the 18th Angel. So she's saying, we sprung from the same source, thus we are just the last Angel. I guess the "ne" is just there to reinforce what the important information in that sentence is. A bit like: "Hey, us humans too!"

[]: Re: Misato dialogue in EoE             Quote Post by Eliaskar » Fri Feb 19, 2016 7:05 pm Ah yes, so that was the mistranslation. It's a shame it's in the subtitles as well, guess i'll just have to deal with it from now on.

[]: Re: Misato dialogue in EoE             Quote Post by Reichu » Fri Feb 19, 2016 7:15 pm robersora wrote: I'd say, the translation is more like Like Adam and Lilith, Humanity has sprung from the same source... We are the 18th Angel.

[]: Re: Misato dialogue in EoE             Quote Post by robersora » Fri Feb 19, 2016 7:53 pm ^ Oh yeah, you're right! Brainfarted pretty hard there. Compared to most Anime, Eva's dialogue is really difficult to parse... And then I get confused. My Power Level is just too low.

[]: Re: Misato dialogue in EoE             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Sat Feb 20, 2016 1:47 am Eliaskar wrote: Ah yes, so that was the mistranslation. It's a shame it's in the subtitles as well, guess i'll just have to deal with it from now on.

[]: Re: Misato dialogue in EoE             Quote Post by Eliaskar » Sat Feb 20, 2016 8:22 am Mr. Tines wrote: There has been a rather better translation around for many years, which I adapted as subtitles for the BD release. See this thread for that and other options.
